user_message: Just got this from Brian Yamamoto at Alpine Ski House -- I need help figuring out security posture. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 13:25 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Alpine Ski House security posture

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 14:22 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Alpine Ski House security posture

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 08:56 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Alpine Ski House

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Alpine Ski House: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-K trap (Dark-customer-outreach module's sequence pattern isn't right for unexpected re-e); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-K failure mode (Dark-customer-outreach module's sequence pattern isn't right for unexpected re-e)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-K -- Dark-customer-outreach module's sequence pattern isn't right for unexpected re-e
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
